questions.insertSentence: These environmentally induced modifications have been documented, in some cases, to persist across two or three subsequent generations without any alteration to the underlying DNA sequence — a finding that sits uncomfortably with strict gene-centric accounts of inheritance.
questions.passage: Classical evolutionary theory, as synthesised in the mid-twentieth century, held that heritable variation could arise only through changes to the nucleotide sequence of DNA. ( ① ) This gene-centric view assigned organisms a largely passive role in their own evolution: environmental modifications of individuals could not be transmitted to offspring. ( ② ) The emergence of epigenetics has complicated this picture by demonstrating that chemical modifications to DNA and its associated histone proteins — alterations that change gene expression without affecting the DNA sequence itself — can be triggered by diet, stress, and toxin exposure. ( ③ ) The implications for evolutionary theory are substantial: if parents transmit not only genes but also environmentally acquired patterns of gene expression, then the mechanisms and tempo of evolutionary change may be considerably more complex than the neo-Darwinian synthesis anticipated. ( ④ ) Defenders of the orthodox view have questioned whether multi-generational transmission effects observed in laboratory conditions occur with sufficient frequency in natural populations to be evolutionarily significant. ( ⑤ ) The debate has prompted calls for an extended evolutionary synthesis that formally incorporates epigenetic inheritance as a distinct channel of biological information transmission.
questions.question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

explanations.correct: 파일상 answer가 3으로 기록(=⑤ 위치)이다. ⑤ 앞 문장은 정통 관점 옹호자들이 '실험실에서 관찰된 세대 간 전달 효과가 자연 집단에서 충분한 빈도로 나타나는지 의문을 제기한다'고 서술한다. 삽입 문장은 'These environmentally induced modifications'로 ③에서 소개된 후성유전적 변형을 받아, 이 변형이 DNA 서열 변화 없이 2~3세대에 걸쳐 지속된다는 구체적 발견을 추가한다. 이것이 바로 옹호자들이 반박하는 핵심 경험적 주장이다. ⑤ 뒤 문장은 이 논쟁이 확장된 진화 종합 이론 요구로 이어졌다고 결론짓는다.
wrong: ①은 오답이다. 'These environmentally induced modifications'가 가리킬 후성유전적 변형이 아직 소개되지 않았다.
②는 오답이다. 환경적 변형이 전달될 수 없다는 유전자 중심 관점의 제약을 서술하는 단계다. 반박 증거를 그 주장 전에 제시하면 순서가 역전된다.
③은 오답이다. 후성유전적 변형이 막 소개되는 단계다. 세대 간 지속이라는 특정 발견이 그 소개를 중간에 끊으면 흐름이 어색하다.
④는 오답이다. ④ 위치에서 진화론적 함의가 서술되는 중이다. 경험적 지속 발견을 이론적 함의 논의 중간에 넣으면 논증이 완결되기 전에 끊긴다.
explanations.key_point: 삽입 문장이 ⑤ 앞 옹호자들의 '빈도 의문'이 겨냥하는 '세대 간 지속' 주장의 구체적 근거를 제공하고, 이 논쟁이 뒤따르는 확장 진화 종합 요구의 동력이 된다.

self_check.correct_answer_reasoning: Before ⑤, defenders of orthodoxy question whether multi-generational transmission effects seen in labs are evolutionarily significant in natural populations. The inserted sentence uses 'These environmentally induced modifications' to refer to the epigenetic changes introduced at ③, and adds the specific claim that they persist across two or three generations without DNA sequence change. This is the key empirical detail that defenders are contesting (persistence in natural populations at sufficient frequency). After ⑤, the passage states that the debate has prompted calls for an extended synthesis — the debate being between the transgenerational persistence evidence (inserted sentence) and the defenders' frequency/magnitude objection (before ⑤). The inserted sentence thus provides the factual basis of the contested claim that motivates the call for an extended synthesis.

position_elimination.①: 'These environmentally induced modifications' has no referent; epigenetics has not been introduced.
position_elimination.②: The gene-centric restriction on transmitting environmental modifications is being stated; the counter-evidence cannot appear before the claim it counters.
position_elimination.③: Epigenetic modifications are just being introduced; the transgenerational persistence finding adds a specific claim that should follow, not interrupt, the introduction.
position_elimination.④: The implications for evolutionary theory are being stated; inserting the empirical persistence finding here disrupts the theoretical-implication argument before it is completed.

questions.insertSentence: Others remain sceptical, maintaining that apparent emergence is simply a reflection of incomplete knowledge of micro-level dynamics and that, in principle, a sufficiently complete analysis of components would always recover the system-level property.
questions.passage: One of the most significant challenges in the philosophy of science is the concept of emergence — the appearance of properties at higher levels of organisation that cannot be straightforwardly derived from the properties of constituent components alone. ( ① ) The wetness of water, the synchronised flashing of fireflies, and the coordinated motion of starling murmurations are all offered as examples of emergent phenomena. ( ② ) Reductionist methodology proceeds on the assumption that understanding a complex system requires decomposing it into its simplest components and characterising their interactions. ( ③ ) Simulation studies of agent-based models have repeatedly produced system-level behaviours — phase transitions, spontaneous ordering, cascading failures — that could not be anticipated by analysing the rules governing individual agents in isolation, suggesting a genuine irreducibility. ( ④ ) This has led some complexity theorists to argue that emergence represents a fundamental limit on the explanatory power of reductionism, not merely a practical difficulty arising from computational intractability. ( ⑤ ) The debate continues to attract contributions from physicists, biologists, and philosophers, reflecting the absence of consensus on whether emergence is an ontological feature of reality or an epistemological artefact of our incomplete models.
questions.question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

explanations.correct: ⑤가 정답이다. ⑤ 앞 문장은 복잡계 이론가들이 '창발이 환원주의의 설명력에 대한 근본적 한계를 나타낸다'는 강한 주장을 제기한다. 삽입 문장은 'Others'로 이 주장에 회의적인 입장을 도입한다: 겉보기 창발은 미시 역학에 대한 불완전한 지식의 반영일 뿐이며, 원리상 완전한 분석이 가능하면 환원주의가 성공한다는 것이다. ⑤ 뒤 문장은 물리학자·생물학자·철학자들 사이의 논쟁이 창발이 존재론적 특성인지 인식론적 인공물인지에 대한 것이라고 마무리하는데, 삽입 문장의 '불완전한 지식(인식론)' 대 '진정한 환원 불가능성(존재론)' 구분이 이 최종 대비와 정확히 대응한다.
wrong: ①은 오답이다. 환원주의나 창발에 대한 입장이 아직 확립되지 않아 회의론자들이 응답할 선행 주장이 없다.
②는 오답이다. 창발 사례들이 나열되는 단계다. 환원주의 도전이 발전되기 전에 회의론적 반론을 삽입하면 이르다.
③은 오답이다. 환원주의 방법론이 묘사되는 단계다. 회의론자들의 방어가 될 수 있지만, 그 방어를 촉발하는 시뮬레이션 도전이 아직 제시되지 않았다.
④는 오답이다. 에이전트 기반 모델 증거는 나왔지만 복잡계 이론가들의 강한 주장(근본적 한계)이 아직 제시되지 않아 회의론자들의 응답이 응답할 대상이 없다.
explanations.key_point: 삽입 문장의 회의론적 'Others'가 ⑤ 앞 복잡계 이론가들의 강한 주장에 대응하고, 뒤따르는 결론의 '존재론 대 인식론' 대비가 삽입 문장의 '진정한 환원 불가능성 대 불완전한 지식' 구분과 정확히 맞물린다.

self_check.correct_answer_reasoning: Before ⑤, the passage presents complexity theorists' argument that emergence is a fundamental limit on reductionism. The inserted sentence introduces 'Others' who are sceptical of this view, arguing that apparent emergence is just incomplete knowledge — reductionism would succeed in principle with full information. After ⑤, the passage notes ongoing debate between physicists, biologists, and philosophers on whether emergence is ontological or epistemological — exactly the debate that the inserted sentence's sceptical position contributes to. The final sentence's contrast between 'ontological feature' and 'epistemological artefact' mirrors the inserted sentence's distinction between genuine irreducibility and incomplete knowledge.

position_elimination.①: No position on reductionism or emergence has been established; the sceptical 'Others' have no prior claim to respond to.
position_elimination.②: Emergence examples are being listed; the sceptical counter-position is premature before the reductionist challenge is developed.
position_elimination.③: Reductionist methodology is described; the sceptics' position is a defence of this methodology, but the simulation challenge that motivates the defence has not yet been presented.
position_elimination.④: The agent-based model evidence for irreducibility has been presented but complexity theorists' strong claim (fundamental limit) has not yet been made; the sceptics' response cannot precede the position it responds to.

questions.insertSentence: Chalmers distinguishes this from what he calls the 'easy problems' — explaining how the brain discriminates stimuli, integrates information, and controls behaviour — which are, in his view, difficult but ultimately tractable by the methods of cognitive neuroscience.
questions.passage: The question of why physical processes in the brain give rise to subjective experience — why there is something it is like to see red, to feel pain, or to hear music — has been described by the philosopher David Chalmers as the 'hard problem of consciousness.' ( ① ) The hard problem is hard because any functional or computational account of the brain's operations seems in principle compatible with the absence of inner experience, leaving open the logical possibility of 'philosophical zombies': beings physically identical to us but devoid of inner life. ( ② ) Neuroscientists working within frameworks such as global workspace theory and integrated information theory have proposed detailed accounts of the neural mechanisms underlying conscious access and reportability. ( ③ ) Chalmers and sympathisers argue, however, that even if these theories correctly identify the neural correlates of consciousness, they do not explain why those correlates should be accompanied by subjective experience rather than occurring 'in the dark.' ( ④ ) This residual explanatory gap has motivated heterodox theoretical positions, from panpsychism — the view that experience is a fundamental feature of matter — to illusionism, which denies the hard problem by arguing that the seeming mysteriousness of subjective experience is itself a cognitive illusion. ( ⑤ ) The debate continues to attract contributions from philosophers, neuroscientists, physicists, and cognitive scientists, reflecting its reputation as perhaps the deepest open question about the nature of mind.
questions.question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

explanations.correct: ②가 정답이다. ② 앞 문장은 '어려운 문제'가 왜 어려운지를 설명한다: 뇌의 기능적 설명이 내적 경험 없이도 원리상 가능하며, '철학적 좀비'의 논리적 가능성이 열려 있다는 것이다. 삽입 문장은 Chalmers가 '쉬운 문제들'(자극 구별·정보 통합·행동 제어)을 구분하는데, 이것들은 인지 신경과학으로 해결 가능하다고 정의한다. ② 뒤 문장들은 신경과학자들이 전지구적 작업공간 이론과 통합 정보 이론으로 의식의 신경 메커니즘을 설명하는데, 이것이 바로 삽입 문장에서 정의한 '쉬운 문제' 접근법이다. 삽입 문장 없이는 독자가 Chalmers가 왜 그 이론들에 불만족하는지 이해할 틀이 없다.
wrong: ①은 오답이다. '어려운 문제'의 명칭만 소개되었을 뿐 아직 왜 어려운지 특성화되지 않았다. '쉽고/어려운' 구분은 어려움 논증이 먼저 나와야 의미 있다.
③은 오답이다. 신경과학자들의 '쉬운 문제' 이론들이 이미 묘사 중이다. 그 이론들이 나온 뒤에 '쉬운/어려운' 구분을 도입하면 순서가 혼란스럽다.
④는 오답이다. Chalmers의 이론 반박이 이미 제시된 상태다. '쉽고/어려운' 프레임은 그 반박이 만들어지기 전에 와야 한다.
⑤는 오답이다. 학제적 논쟁으로 마무리되는 결론이다. 정의적 구분은 결론이 아닌 논증 본론에 속한다.
explanations.key_point: 삽입 문장의 '쉬운 문제' 정의가 ② 뒤 신경과학자 이론들의 성격을 규정하고, ③에서 Chalmers가 '그 이론들이 맞더라도 어려운 문제는 남는다'고 반박할 수 있는 개념적 틀을 제공한다.

self_check.correct_answer_reasoning: Before ②, the passage explains the hard problem and why it is hard (zombie argument). The inserted sentence defines the contrast class — 'easy problems' — which are tractable by cognitive neuroscience. After ②, the passage describes neuroscientists working on global workspace theory and IIT — these are precisely the theories addressing the 'easy problems' defined in the inserted sentence. Chalmers' objection in ③ ('even if these theories correctly identify the neural correlates...') then makes sense as the hard problem reasserting itself against easy-problem solutions. Without the inserted sentence, the reader has no framework for understanding why Chalmers is dissatisfied with the neuroscientists' accounts.

position_elimination.①: The hard problem has been named but not yet characterised as hard; the easy/hard distinction requires the hardness argument first.
position_elimination.③: Neuroscientists' easy-problem theories are already being described; introducing the easy/hard distinction after the theories appear confuses the sequence.
position_elimination.④: Chalmers' objection to functional theories is already stated; the easy/hard framework belongs before the objection it frames.
position_elimination.⑤: Interdisciplinary debate concludes the passage; the definitional distinction belongs in the argumentative body.

questions.topic: infrastructural path dependency and technology lock-in
questions.insertSentence: This lock-in effect is compounded by the network externalities that characterise many infrastructure systems: each additional user of a technology increases its value to all existing users, creating a self-reinforcing dynamic that makes defection from the dominant standard costly even when superior alternatives are available.
questions.passage: The concept of path dependency holds that the options available to decision-makers at any moment are constrained by choices made in the past, particularly when those choices involved building durable physical or institutional infrastructure. ( ① ) The persistence of the QWERTY keyboard layout, the gauge of railway tracks, and the design of electrical grids all illustrate how early technological commitments create trajectories that subsequent decisions must follow. ( ② ) The economic mechanism underlying path dependency is often described in terms of switching costs: once a technology is widely adopted and complementary assets — trained workers, maintenance infrastructure, compatible equipment — have been built around it, transitioning requires dismantling and rebuilding the entire ecosystem of complementary investments. ( ③ ) The case of hydrogen fuel cell vehicles illustrates this dynamic: despite potential advantages in certain use cases, their diffusion has been slowed by the absence of a refuelling network, while the battery electric vehicle ecosystem accumulates exactly the kind of complementary infrastructure that creates path dependency. ( ④ ) Policymakers who wish to redirect technological trajectories cannot rely on price signals and market competition alone; they must actively disrupt self-reinforcing dynamics through mechanisms such as mandatory interoperability standards, subsidies for complementary infrastructure, and coordinated industry agreements. ( ⑤ ) Without such interventions, the transition to more sustainable or efficient technologies may be indefinitely delayed by the inertia of embedded investments.
questions.question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

explanations.correct: ④가 정답이다. ④ 앞 문장은 수소차 대 배터리 전기차 사례를 제시하는데, 전기차 생태계가 보완 인프라를 축적하면서 경로 의존성이 만들어지는 과정을 보여준다. 삽입 문장은 'This lock-in effect'로 이 사례에서 드러난 고착 효과를 받아 두 번째 심화 메커니즘인 네트워크 외부성을 추가한다: 사용자가 늘수록 기술의 가치가 모든 기존 사용자에게 높아져, 더 나은 대안이 있어도 지배적 표준을 이탈하기 어려운 자기 강화 역학이 작동한다. ④ 뒤 문장은 정책 입안자들이 '자기 강화 역학'을 능동적으로 교란해야 한다고 권고하는데, 이 자기 강화 역학이 바로 삽입 문장의 네트워크 외부성이다.
wrong: ①은 오답이다. 경로 의존성이 개념적으로만 소개되었고 구체적 고착 메커니즘이나 사례가 없어 'This lock-in effect'의 선행사가 없다.
②는 오답이다. 역사적 사례들이 제시되었지만 전환 비용이 아직 정의되지 않아 네트워크 외부성 도입이 이중으로 이르다.
③은 오답이다. 전환 비용은 정의되었지만 'This lock-in effect'가 지시하는 자동차 사례가 아직 등장하지 않았다.
⑤는 오답이다. 정책 결론과 '내장된 투자의 관성'이라는 아이디어가 마무리를 이루는 단계다. 메커니즘 설명을 결론 뒤에 삽입하면 구조가 무너진다.
explanations.key_point: 'This lock-in effect'가 ④ 앞 전기차 사례의 보완 생태계 고착을 받고, 삽입 문장의 '네트워크 외부성' 자기 강화 메커니즘이 뒤따르는 정책 처방의 '자기 강화 역학 교란' 목표물이 된다.

self_check.correct_answer_reasoning: Before ④, the passage presents the hydrogen vs. battery electric vehicle case, which illustrates switching costs and how a growing complementary ecosystem creates path dependency (sentence before ④). The inserted sentence uses 'This lock-in effect' to refer to the lock-in demonstrated by the automotive example and adds a second compounding mechanism: network externalities, where each additional user increases value to all, making defection from the dominant standard costly even when better alternatives exist. After ④, the passage turns to policy responses aimed at disrupting 'self-reinforcing dynamics' — the self-reinforcing dynamic is precisely the network externality mechanism introduced in the inserted sentence. 'This lock-in effect' requires the preceding automotive example as its referent.

position_elimination.①: Path dependency has been introduced conceptually; no specific lock-in mechanism or example has been given.
position_elimination.②: Historical examples are presented but switching costs have not yet been defined; network externalities are doubly premature.
position_elimination.③: Switching costs have been defined but the automotive example that 'This lock-in effect' refers to has not yet appeared.
position_elimination.⑤: Policy conclusion and the 'inertia of embedded investments' idea close the passage; inserting the mechanism description at the very end disrupts the conclusion.

questions.topic: anthropological kinship systems and classificatory logic
questions.insertSentence: Descriptive systems, characteristic of many Western European societies, employ a unique term for each distinct biological or marital relationship, distinguishing, for example, between a paternal uncle and a maternal uncle, or between a half-sibling and a full sibling.
questions.passage: Anthropologists studying kinship systems across cultures have found that the categories used to organise descent, marriage, and siblinghood vary so significantly that terms borrowed from one system consistently misrepresent the structure of another. ( ① ) The most fundamental analytical distinction introduced to address this problem is between descriptive and classificatory kinship systems. ( ② ) Classificatory systems, by contrast, group under a single term individuals who occupy the same structural position in the kinship network, regardless of their precise genealogical relationship. ( ③ ) Thus, in a society organised on classificatory principles, one's biological father and all of his brothers may be addressed by the same term, not because the system fails to distinguish individuals but because it classifies them as occupying an equivalent social position with respect to the speaker. ( ④ ) Lewis Henry Morgan, who first systematically mapped this distinction in the nineteenth century, argued that classificatory terminologies reflect a social logic of group solidarity: the same term for father and father's brothers encodes the fact that all these men share a common set of rights and obligations toward the speaker. ( ⑤ ) Contemporary kinship theorists have largely moved beyond the evolutionary framework Morgan attached to this insight, but his core observation that kinship categories encode social relationships rather than merely map biological facts remains foundational to the field.
questions.question: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳을 고르시오.

explanations.correct: ②가 정답이다. ② 앞 문장은 기술적(descriptive) 친족 체계와 분류적(classificatory) 친족 체계의 구분이 핵심 분석 도구로 도입된다고 서술한다. 삽입 문장은 '기술적 체계'의 편을 구체적 예시(부계 삼촌 vs. 모계 삼촌, 이복 형제 vs. 친형제 구분)와 함께 정의한다. ② 뒤 문장은 'by contrast'로 분류적 체계를 정의한다: 동일한 구조적 위치를 점하는 개인들을 단일 용어로 묶는다는 것이다. 'by contrast'는 대비할 선행 개념이 필요하며, 삽입 문장의 기술적 체계 정의가 그 선행 개념이다.
wrong: ①은 오답이다. '기술적/분류적' 구분이 아직 소개되지 않아 한쪽 편을 정의하기 이르다.
③은 오답이다. ③ 위치에서는 분류적 체계가 'by contrast'로 이미 정의되는 중이다. 여기에 기술적 체계 정의를 삽입하면 'by contrast'가 문법적으로 중복된다.
④는 오답이다. 분류적 체계의 논리적 함의(아버지와 아버지의 형제를 같은 용어로 부르는 이유)를 도출하는 단계다. 이 시점에서 기술적 체계의 기초 정의는 어울리지 않는다.
⑤는 오답이다. Morgan의 기여와 현대 친족 이론의 수용이라는 역사적·이론적 마무리 단계다. 정의 쌍은 역사적 맥락화 이전에 와야 한다.
explanations.key_point: 삽입 문장의 '기술적 체계' 정의가 ② 뒤 'by contrast'(분류적 체계 정의)의 대비 대상을 제공한다. 삽입 문장 없이는 'by contrast'가 대비할 선행 개념이 없어 문맥이 불완전하다.

self_check.correct_answer_reasoning: Before ②, the passage introduces the descriptive vs. classificatory distinction as the key analytical tool. The inserted sentence defines the 'descriptive' side of this pairing with concrete examples (paternal vs. maternal uncle; half vs. full sibling). After ②, the passage uses 'by contrast' to define the classificatory side — grouping structurally equivalent individuals under one term. The 'by contrast' requires a prior term to contrast with; the inserted sentence's definition of descriptive systems is that prior term. Without the inserted sentence, 'by contrast' at ③ has nothing to contrast against.

position_elimination.①: The descriptive/classificatory distinction has not yet been introduced; defining one pole is premature.
position_elimination.③: Classificatory systems are being defined; inserting the descriptive definition here after 'by contrast' would make 'by contrast' grammatically redundant.
position_elimination.④: The logical implication of classificatory systems is being drawn; the foundational definition of descriptive systems is out of place here.
position_elimination.⑤: Morgan's contribution and contemporary reception conclude the passage; the definitional pair should appear before the historical and theoretical elaboration.
